---
title: Snippets
---

Snippets are reusable text fragments that you can insert into any document. They are useful when you find yourself copying the same block of content --- a callout, a disclaimer, a standard methods section --- across multiple pages. Instead of duplicating the text, write it once as a snippet file and reference it by name.


# Syntax

Insert a snippet with {% raw %}`{{ snip.name }}`{% endraw %}, where `name` matches the file name (without extension):

{% raw %}
```
{{ snip.source_tip }}
```
{% endraw %}

Use underscores in snippet names, since Jinja dot-access does not allow dashes.


# Where to store snippets

Snippet files live in a `snippets/` directory at the project root, organized into subdirectories by format:

```
snippets/
  common/          # format-agnostic (.jinja extension)
  html/            # HTML-specific (.html extension)
  latex/           # LaTeX-specific (.tex extension)
  typst/           # Typst-specific (.typ extension)
```


# Resolution order

When *Calepin* encounters {% raw %}`{{ snip.source_tip }}`{% endraw %}, it looks for a file in this order (first match wins):

1. **Target-specific** --- `snippets/{target}/source_tip.{ext}` (e.g., `snippets/website/source_tip.html` when rendering the `website` target)
2. **Base-specific** --- `snippets/{base}/source_tip.{ext}` (e.g., `snippets/html/source_tip.html`)
3. **Format-agnostic** --- `snippets/common/source_tip.jinja`

Most snippets only need a single file in `common/`. Use the format-specific directories when the content must differ between output formats --- for example, when it contains raw HTML or LaTeX.


# Examples

## A reusable callout

File `snippets/common/source_tip.jinja`:

{% raw %}
````
::: {.callout-tip}
::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
Click the **</>** button in the menu at the top of this page to see the `.qmd` source code for this example.
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="latex"}
The source code for this page is available at: <https://vincentarelbundock.github.io/calepin>
:::
:::
````
{% endraw %}

Then in any document:

{% raw %}
```
{{ snip.source_tip }}
```
{% endraw %}

## A standard disclaimer with metadata

Snippets go through normal Jinja processing after expansion, so they can reference front matter fields and variables:

File `snippets/common/draft_notice.jinja`:

{% raw %}
````
::: {.callout-warning}
## Draft

This is a draft of *{{ meta.title }}*. Do not cite or circulate.
:::
````
{% endraw %}

## Format-specific content

When the same snippet needs fundamentally different markup per format, use the format-specific directories. For example, to embed a custom HTML widget in HTML output and a static image in LaTeX:

File `snippets/html/widget.html`:

```html
<div id="interactive-widget">…</div>
```

File `snippets/latex/widget.tex`:

```latex
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{fig/widget-static.pdf}
```

Both are accessed with the same call: {% raw %}`{{ snip.widget }}`{% endraw %}.
